compiled_state.title: Figma design collaboration: Push via SVG export and revision image
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Context

DSO's design workflow is fully autonomous — design-wireframe produces a 3-artifact manifest (spatial-layout.json, wireframe.svg, tokens.md) reviewed by LLM-based design reviewers. Teams with a human designer have no structured way to collaborate. This epic adds the first half of a Figma collaboration loop: DSO's wireframe.svg is optimized for Figma import, the user imports it into Figma for the designer to review and revise, the user exports a revision PNG and places it in the project, and the implementation agent uses the original manifest (for behavioral specs) alongside the revision image (for visual intent).

## Success Criteria

1. When design.figma_collaboration=true (opt-in per design), wireframe.svg is written with Figma-optimized structure (semantic group names, element IDs matching spatial-layout.json), and the story is tagged design:awaiting_import with instructions for the user
2. User exports PNG from Figma to designs/{uuid}/figma-revision.png, runs approval command that validates file exists at path before replacing tag with design:approved
3. Sprint skill skips design:awaiting_import stories and surfaces them as "Awaiting designer input" in status reports
4. Implementation agent uses original manifest (authoritative for behavior) + revision image (authoritative for visual layout/styling); manifest governs behavior, image governs visual; contradictions flagged as [NEEDS_REVIEW]
5. No effect on projects without the flag

## Scenario Analysis

13 surviving scenarios. Key risks: PNG at wrong path (mitigation: approval validates file exists), implementation agent multimodal capability (mitigation: specify model tier), SVG/JSON ID divergence (mitigation: shared ID registry), worktree lifecycle for designs/ (mitigation: explicit git policy), permanent awaiting_import if designer abandons (mitigation: staleness warning), manifest/image out of sync after re-run (mitigation: guard regeneration when approved).

## Dependencies

None beyond existing DSO infrastructure. No external APIs, no npm packages, no Figma account required from DSO's side.

## Approach

Minimal-infrastructure collaboration loop. DSO's existing wireframe.svg is optimized for Figma import. The designer imports SVG, revises in Figma, user exports revision PNG. Implementation agent uses dual-source strategy: manifest for structure/behavior, image for visual intent. Zero API integration, zero auth, zero external dependencies. Establishes the workflow pattern that the Pull epics later automate.

## Research Artifacts

- .claude/artifacts/figma-integration-research-guide.md
- .claude/artifacts/design-format-research-synthesis.md
- .claude/artifacts/figma-feasibility-synthesis.md
- .claude/artifacts/design-manifest-pull-format-analysis.md

### Planning Intelligence Log

- **Web research**: triggered (8 research agents: Figma MCP/API, GitHub integrations, alternatives, design intent for LLMs, agentic projects, custom plugin, REST API, lateral thinking)
- **Scenario analysis**: triggered (17 red team → 13 surviving blue team)
- **Practitioner-requested additional cycles**: web research re-run 2x (feasibility investigation + 5-agent parallel alternatives investigation)
